revisions.comment: I restored the previous version. The words are attributed and can be used and provide important context.

proper translitered name - re [[Special:Diff/1239160983|1239160983]] - the authors were from [[The Paper (newspaper)|The Paper]] which is from [[Mainland China]] - [[Large language model]] probably couldn't differentiate between [[hanja]], [[kanji]], [[chu nom]] and even more complicated couldn't differentiate [[Standard Chinese|standard mandarin chinese]], [[Hong Kong Cantonese]], [[Taiwanese Hokkien]], [[Taiwanese Hakka]] - also [[mos:ZH]] usually without [[tone mark]]

Revert. You'll need a better source for this than Anadolu. Per [[WP:RSPLIST]]: "In the 2019 RfC, editors generally agreed that Anadolu Agency is generally unreliable for topics that are controversial or related to international politics."

v2.05 - Fix errors for [[WP:WCW|CW project]] (Break in list - Template value ends with break - Spelling and typography)
Rewrite caption, see talk. "An aerial view of the massacre captured by an Israeli drone. Aid trucks and Palestinian pedestrians (some dead) are visible."

Al Jazeera did not make an official editorial, but a correspondent did. Changing to reflect that.
Its not alleged anymore, its unfortunately the reality.

keeping phrasing that clearly identifies victims as Palestinian
CNN doesn't make such a statement. I assume it's talking about the first sentence, which is about "obstruction" rather than "attacks", and also not in CNN's voice, but attributed to interviewees
"narrative" seems like editorializing, "version of events" is more dispassionate and matches the source
Describe those harmed as "Palestinians". "Civilians" is probably roughly accurate also, but it's not the word the sources use
removed [[Category:War crimes in the Israel–Hamas war]]; added [[Category:Israeli war crimes in the Israel–Hamas war]] using [[WP:HC|HotCat]]
/* top */ Performed minor cleanup and wikification.
Cleaned up references and other matters, including deleting URL cruft.

Undid revision [[Special:Diff/1222844271|1222844271]] by [[Special:Contributions/Utilisateur19911|Utilisateur19911]] ([[User talk:Utilisateur19911|talk]]) This was originally added and reverted, ONUS  is on those adding the material to demonstrate consensus, discuss at talk.

Reverted per WP:BRD. Please seek talk-page consensus before deleting material about which there is no evidence-based dispute. There is no such thing as an MOS "violation" — the MOS is a guide, not a statute. The content is copiously and reliably sourced, so the mysterious invocation of WP:BURDEN also needs to be explained.

removing recently added mass blocks of text introduced to the lede that does not summarize the body, does not directly relate to the massacre in question, and violates [[MOS:LEDE]] of having four maximum paragraphs. Please seek talk page consensus before reinserting disputed material per [[WP:BRD]] and [[WP:BURDEN]]
After some reflection I have moved the point about aid-seekers being killed to its own paragraph to avoid the non sequitur - readability also matters.
Notifying subject page of move discussion on [[Talk:Flour massacre#Requested move 28 April 2024|Talk:Flour massacre]]
Rescuing 36 sources and tagging 0 as dead.) #IABot (v2.0.9.5
Yet again, the number of videos supporting the earlier timing is one, so no plural.
Having raised the problem that the article may give the impression it was a planned aid distribution twice on the talk page in recent weeks to no effect, I have added clarifying context myself.
/* top */ Infobox is best for undisputed facts, leave the time parameter out, the dispute is covered in the article.

Undid revision [[Special:Diff/1219352772|1219352772]] by [[Special:Contributions/Utilisateur19911|Utilisateur19911]] ([[User talk:Utilisateur19911|talk]]) no idea why CNN analysis was removed; there wasn't just some survivors' footage, this footage was verified; removing this info from infobox is misleading

Removed CNN from Infobox, which is not the place for debate. See: every other Infobox containing different claims by different participants. The 4:22 timing is supported by one video — avoid creative pluralisation.
redirect bypass from [[5.56x45mm NATO]] to [[5.56×45mm NATO]] using [[:en:Wikipedia:Tools/Navigation_popups|popups]]

opening paragraph must establish context per MOS:OPEN
no need for this disclaimer as the refs are abundant in the first sentence
the opening paragraph must be kept general per MOS:OPEN
/* Palestinian human rights organizations */ expansion; this deserves much more attention that one sentence
/* Israeli Defense Forces */ added CNN analysis of eyewitnesses
Carrying not providing aid. "Providing" crosses the line from euphemism to invention.

Detangle official positions. No one directly quotes the Gaza Ministry that I can find, so their casualty numbers are being attached to a gunfire description they don't make. Likewise IDF accounts do not say the stampede was precipitated by the warning shots.
begin lead narrative with journalists on the scene. These witnesses, whose accounts appear in the body, may be Palestinian, but I don't know that, so feel free to add that detail if confirmed.
IDF is the disputing party; cite.
Improve sourcing and specificity of gunshot victims in lead.
/* Israeli Defense Forces */ request for unedited footage declined
say who calls it a massacre per sources in body; move tag with edit
/* Israeli Defense Forces */ add different Israeli denial
"dub" normally works like "call" ("called the incident a massacre") and not like "describe" ("described the incident as a massacre"), so no "as" required.
There seems to be consensus on the talk page that the potential claim in question is not problematic. Unless a new point of concern is brought up, the NPOV tag does not need to stay.
Mentioned that the label "massacre" is disputed.
/* International */This bit does not seem as important, since both sources are primary. If it was important enough to include, there would be secondary sources.

Removing POV tag, the article simply does not have a significant POV problem, and the talk page justification for the tag is not specific enough or actionable. There is also already a 'current event' tag and the article has been and will continue to change drastically. Specific POV issues can be dealt with by improving them directly or by discussing them.
